历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for Physics were 33819 → 30650 → 32434 → 28281 → 28751, showing a fluctuating but generally advancing trend. In 2024, it advanced significantly to 28281, then slightly retreated to 28751 in 2025, stabilizing around the 28,000–29,000 range in recent two years. The baseline ranking for 2025 is approximately 28751. The enrollment plan for this university in Chongqing Physics was 51 in 2021, 53 in 2022, 52 in 2023, 63 in 2024, and 72 in 2025, showing a continuous expansion trend. The 2026 plan is 77, an increase of 5 (+6.9%) compared to 2025, which slightly eases competition and tends to push rankings backward.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: China University of Petroleum (Beijing) is a 211 Project and Double First-Class university, with its main campus ranking approximately 48–62 on the Soft Science main list. The Karamay Campus shares the same educational system as the main campus, enjoying the 211/Double First-Class status, and has high recognition among science and engineering institutions. There are no significant events in reputation sentiment, Douyin popularity, or abnormal application records, indicating a neutral and stable reputation that does not significantly positively or negatively impact application psychology.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Geography
专业热度与地域.text: The social popularity of majors such as Petroleum Engineering and Computer Science is generally stable. The total number of candidates for the 2026 Chongqing Gaokao is approximately 209,000 (no subject-specific data). Based on the recent Physics proportion of about 66.7%, the estimated number of Physics candidates is about 139,000, roughly the same as the 139,500 in 2025, with no significant fluctuation in candidate scale. The university is located in Karamay, Xinjiang, far from Chongqing, which somewhat suppresses the willingness of Chongqing candidates to apply due to geographical preference, but the 211/Double First-Class status still attracts interest.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 28751, the 6.9% enrollment expansion in 2026 pushing rankings backward by about 1200 places, the roughly stable total number of candidates, and neutral reputation, the predicted minimum ranking for Physics in 2026 is approximately 29969, with a range of 29469–30469.

If your ranking is significantly better than the lower limit of the range (e.g., within 29000), you can focus on aiming for Tier 1 flagship majors.
